channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Security Forces Heighten Operations in BARMM Amid Election Violence Fears
item.description: Security forces in the Bangsamoro region have intensified operations as the autonomous region begins its first parliamentary election period, amid fears of election-related violence spilling over into...

item.title: The Spectator's Islamophobic Coverage and Its Impact on British Media
item.description: Nesrine Malik critiques the Spectator, Britain's oldest political magazine, for its systematic promotion of Islamophobia. A comprehensive report by the Centre for Media Monitoring analysed 3,733 artic...

item.title: Oshwal Academy: Details on Fees, Curriculum, Location, and Parent Resources
item.description: Oshwal Academy is a leading international school in Kenya with branches in Nairobi and Mombasa, offering the British National Curriculum. The school caters to learners aged 1.5 to 18, divided into kin...

item.title: Cameroon's Gold Exports: Challenges in Tracking Illicit Trade
item.description: The article investigates the significant issue of Cameroon's gold exports, revealing that only about 5% of the country's gold production is legally exported. The majority of the gold leaves the countr...

item.title: Dr. Toyosi Akerele-Ogunsiji urges governments to invest in AI education and research
item.description: Dr. Toyosi Akerele-Ogunsiji, an AI governance expert, urged governments to invest more in universities and equip lecturers with tools to drive technological innovation. She emphasized that AI will tra...

item.title: Spain's Defensive Mastery Secures Second World Cup Title
item.description: Spain's historic second FIFA World Cup victory over Argentina in the 2026 tournament was celebrated with emphasis on their unparalleled defensive record. Coach Luis de la Fuente's team conceded just o...

item.title: Akinwumi Adesina's Continued Leadership in African Development Initiatives
item.description: Akinwumi Adesina, the former president of the African Development Bank (AfDB), has swiftly transitioned into new leadership roles after leaving his position. Just months post-AfDB, he assumed the chai...

item.title: Alfred Meyer Challenges New York Times' Radiation Safety Narrative
item.description: Alfred Meyer, a prominent figure in Physicians for Social Responsibility, criticizes a New York Times article that advocates for relaxing radiation safety regulations to boost nuclear energy expansion...

item.title: Kenyans Turn Football Screening into Political Protest at KICC
item.description: Hundreds of Kenyans gathered at Nairobi's KICC for a public screening of the Spain vs Argentina World Cup match, creating a vibrant atmosphere. However, the event took an unexpected political turn as ...

item.description: The article reports that World Cup viewing parties in Vancouver were at full capacity for the final match, highlighting the city's enthusiastic support for the tournament. Published on July 19, 2026, ...

item.title: Alcohol use linked to sexual violence and HIV risk among young women in South Africa

item.title: Transport Groups Demand P2 Fare Hike Amid Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Transport groups in the Philippines are demanding a P2 fare increase and considering a nationwide strike due to the surge in fuel prices caused by geopolitical tensions between the United States and I...

item.title: Ganduje Warns Obi and Atiku Lack Grassroots Support to Challenge Tinubu in 2027 Election

item.title: NRC Proposes Relaxing Radiation Exposure Limits to Cut Costs and Pleasing Donors
item.description: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has proposed a draft rule allowing nuclear power plants to increase radiation exposure limits at their discretion, prioritizing cost savings over safety. C...

item.title: ADHD Medication Risks and Long-Term Heart Effects
item.description: Dr Max Pemberton, a psychiatrist, warns about the long-term cardiovascular risks associated with ADHD medication. He recounts a case of a patient who suffered a cardiac arrest while on ADHD drugs, hig...

item.title: Bald eagle Jackie rescued near Big Bear after ground fight with sub-adults
item.description: A bald eagle believed to be Jackie, one-half of the iconic Big Bear nest livestream pair, was rescued near Big Bear Lake after being found on the ground in a fight with two sub-adult eagles. The adult...
